reasoning: The expected natural market drift is positive overall, but NTRS has a declared ex-dividend date on September 5, 2025, for about $0.80 per share. The resolution threshold is set to the highly elevated recent close of $128.96. The dividend will structurally pull the unadjusted close price downwards. For the shortest term (Sept 7 and Sept 30), baseline positive equity drift acts too slowly to outpace the instant ~$0.80 drop, positioning the expected price slightly below the starting threshold and hence pushing the probability below 0.5. As the forecast horizon extends to November and February, positive drift (approximately 6-8% annualized) outweighs the dividend drops, shifting the medians back into positive territory. However, adjusting for the mandated requirement to push far-horizon probabilities strictly toward 0.5 as uncertainty grows limits the long-term probabilities to 0.52 and 0.51. Furthermore, current analyst assessments describing the stock as somewhat overvalued following a massive 4-month rally support a modest containment of the positive expected return.
